displayTitle: Two Spectra, Two Knobs, One Rule

beats.narration: Source on one side, film on the other, part between. The emerging beam carries a map of everything it passed through. That's radiography.

payload.title: KNOW YOUR BEAM
payload.sub: Every radiograph is a map drawn by the beam.

beats.narration: X-rays and gamma rays sit at the spectrum's short end. Shorter wavelength means higher energy: a harder, more penetrating beam. That's quality.
An X-ray tube emits a continuous spread capped at the kilovoltage you set. Gamma isotopes emit only fixed lines. No knob tunes them.

payload.heading: Two spectra: continuous vs. line
payload.note: NO KNOB CHANGES A NUCLEAR ENERGY LEVEL

beats.narration: Two knobs. Milliamperage sets electron current: double it, intensity doubles, quality unchanged. Kilovoltage hardens the beam and raises intensity together.
The rule: milliamperage sets how much, quantity only. Kilovoltage sets how hard, quality and quantity together. Master both and exposure charts make sense.

payload.sub: Then every exposure chart makes sense.
